blocking_issues: Highest risk: `canonical_state_integrity` did not run locally; confirm canonical state before trusting the run summary.
recommended_next_actions: UI review note: the field-manual shell now auto-opens the targeted collapsed step card when a top overview link lands on that step, and each collapsed step summary now shows a literal per-step command count such as `6 commands`. Use the live preview to confirm that both cues feel natural and useful in a real support scan.

UI review note: the field-manual top step list now uses dedicated `overview_summary` text instead of recycled action prose. Review the live `ASMS UI is down` and `ASMS Keycloak auth is down` pages in the preview to judge whether the new quick-look wording is concise enough for real support scanning, and tune the summaries further if any still sound too formal or too abstract.

UI review note: the shared field-manual shell now emits playbook-local overview anchors, so the `ASMS UI is down` summary-step links should land on the matching command sections in the live preview instead of a missing route. Use the current preview pass to confirm that operator navigation now feels reliable before judging more wording and shell details.

Immediate next action: use the completed flow-first publication contract wave as the new publication baseline. The new contract plus the revised page-type and support-language notes now match the real support workflow better, so the next pass should judge Keycloak and the remaining boundary playbooks against that tighter baseline instead of the older wrapper-heavy assumptions.

Target-helper note: the saved `algosec-lab` profile remains the working bounded proof path under `dist/candidates/adf-target-profile-baseline/`. In the latest slice the target-backed site renders 5 playbooks and 3 guides, including the generated `asms-runtime-taxonomy` guide, on top of the already-proven 4 diagnostic flows, 6 page records, 4 generated decision playbooks, and 8 symptom routes.

Taxonomy note: `docs/specs/adf-cross-playbook-system-layer-taxonomy-v1.md` is now the canonical shared ADF layer map. Use it to keep the next content pass ASMS-runtime-first, and treat host integration plus Linux operating evidence as supporting surfaces rather than the first architectural layer.

Code-anchor note: the five session-created refinement specs now explicitly name the current `runtime_baseline.py` and `starlight_site.py` functions expected to change first, so the next implementation pass should start from those code anchors instead of re-deriving migration surfaces.

Recommended next action: keep `deepen_dependency_aware_playbooks` active, but drive the next content pass through the page-type, support-language translation, symptom-to-boundary mapping, support-safe scientific-method, and backend map-and-render specs before refining more operator-facing Starlight content.

Adversarial review note: the refinement specs were tightened to fail closed on page-type mixing, internal architecture jargon in the frontline flow, and open-ended lab walks. Keep those tightenings in force when touching generator or content code next, and use `eval/history/adf-frontline-refinement-adversarial-review-20260330.md` as the summary note.

Second adversarial review note: the scientific-method and backend-map notes were tightened to fail closed on theory-heavy drift, service-only bias, renderer guessing, and unsafe schema migration shortcuts. Use `eval/history/adf-scientific-method-and-backend-map-adversarial-review-20260330.md` as the summary note for those two contracts.

Method note: use `docs/specs/adf-support-safe-scientific-method-contract-v1.md` so ADF content keeps a clear observe -> hypothesis -> test -> result -> next branch loop instead of drifting into broad subsystem theory.

Backend note: use `docs/specs/adf-backend-map-and-render-contract-v1.md` so the Codex/backend path builds structured mapping rows and page records before Starlight rendering instead of improvising support pages from free-form analysis.

Pilot note: the official staged observe-only slice against target `10.167.2.150` now validates the imported-module boundary packet method, but only as a pilot. Keep it easy to pivot away from if later modules need too much special branching.

Contract note: use `docs/specs/adf-flow-first-operator-publishing-contract-v1.md`, `docs/specs/adf-frontline-page-types-and-entry-contract-v1.md`, `docs/specs/adf-frontline-support-language-translation-contract-v1.md`, and `docs/specs/adf-symptom-to-boundary-mapping-workflow-v1.md` together so systems thinking stays in the discovery model while frontline pages stay fast and readable for support engineers.

Workflow caveat: the remote slice produced a real checkpoint bundle and run summary, but the current wrapper/export step did not emit a local runtime-evidence bundle. Treat the preserved local review copy under `.pack-state/local-scratch/remote-autonomy-roundtrips/.../incoming/remote-run-audit-only/` as audit-only scratch until a canonical import path succeeds.

Remote-loop note: `docs/specs/adf-remote-autonomy-testing-workflow-v1.md` now treats `starter_backlog_incomplete` as retired wording. Judge future `adf-dev` terminal records in current-task terms such as `current_task_incomplete`, `task_slice_complete`, and `blocked_boundary`.

Validation contract reminder: use `docs/specs/adf-imported-module-drilldown-pilot-v1.md` together with the existing ADF mapping and validation notes so Keycloak follow-up stays evidence-first, general enough to reuse, and easy to abandon if the pilot does not pay off.

Planning note: the adf-dev execution transition is now a named planning line in `docs/specs/adf-three-node-autonomy-contract-v1.md`, and the future remote-canonical mode remains explicitly future-only in `docs/specs/adf-remote-canonical-home-transition-spec-v1.md`.
